title: Transport block transmission using different spatial parameters
abstract: Various aspects of the present disclosure generally relate to wireless communication. In some aspects, a wireless communication device may determine a first set of parameters associated with a first transport block (TB) repetition of a TB and a second set of parameters associated with a second TB repetition of the TB; and determine a TB size of the TB based at least in part on the first set of parameters, the second set of parameters, or both the first set of parameters and the second set of parameters. Numerous other aspects are provided.
claims: 1. A method of wireless communication performed by a user equipment (UE), comprising: receiving a grant for a first transport block (TB) repetition, wherein the grant indicates a first mini-slot in which the first TB repetition is scheduled; receiving an indication of one or more spatial parameters corresponding to one or more subsequent TB repetitions; and determining one or more subsequent mini-slots, that occur after the first mini-slot, in which the one or more subsequent TB repetitions are scheduled based at least in part on a mini-slot pattern configuration or one or more parameters of the first TB repetition.
